summary: DHH announces a two-hour presentation on Ruby on Rails and Basecamp at Roskilde University Center. The talk will cover a one-hour introduction to Ruby, Rails, and Basecamp followed by a one-hour Rails tutorial. This early 2004 post captures a pivotal moment just before Rails was publicly released, when DHH was still evangelizing the framework through university presentations. The post was later updated with a link to the official Ruby on Rails release.
key_points: DHH scheduled a free two-hour presentation splitting time between Ruby/Rails introduction and a hands-on Rails tutorial
The presentation was hosted at Roskilde University Center in Denmark, reflecting Rails' Danish origins before its global adoption
The post was retroactively updated to announce that Ruby on Rails had been officially released, marking a historic moment in web development
money_quotes: Ruby on Rails has been released!
It'll be Thursday, May 6th at 13.00-15.00 in Biografen 41.1, Roskilde Universitetscenter. It's free to come and there's plenty of space.
key_insight: This brief announcement captures the humble, grassroots origins of Ruby on Rails — a free university presentation in Denmark — just before it became one of the most influential web frameworks in history.
